name: Observed Baryonic Spectrum (Physically Derived)
description: Real cosmological number densities by epoch, derived from the flat LambdaCDM Friedmann equation (Planck 2018 parameters: H0=67.4 km/s/Mpc, Omega_m=0.315, Omega_Lambda=0.685, Omega_r=9.18e-5) and standard particle-physics anchor values, NOT pulled from an unverified third party.

methodology.z_of_t: Numerically integrated t(a) = int_0^a da'/(a' H0 E(a')) with E(a)=sqrt(Omega_r a^-4 + Omega_m a^-3 + Omega_Lambda), inverted for a(t). Validated against the well-measured recombination redshift: this calculation gives z=1077 at t=379,000 yr, within ~1% of the standard z~1089, and a total computed age of 13.79 Gyr vs. the measured 13.787 Gyr.
methodology.L1_neutrinos: n(t) = n0 * (1+z(t))^3, exact (1+z)^3 dilution. Valid because total neutrino number is conserved after decoupling (~1s, T~1MeV), well before any of these table entries. n0 = 3.36e8 /m^3, the standard literature value for the total relic neutrino+antineutrino number density today, summed over all 3 flavors (~336/cm^3).
methodology.L2_hadrons: n(t) = n0 * (1+z(t))^3, exact (1+z)^3 dilution. Valid because baryon number is exactly conserved after baryogenesis, well before BBN. n0 = 0.25 /m^3, derived from Omega_b*h^2 = 0.0224 (Planck 2018): rho_b,0 = Omega_b_h2 * 1.878e-26 kg/m^3 / h^2 ... divided by the proton mass.
methodology.L3_atoms: n(t) = n2(t) * neutral_fraction(z(t)). neutral_fraction = 1 - x_e(z), where x_e(z) is an ionization-fraction model with TWO transitions: recombination (ionized -> neutral) centered z=1089, width 80 in z; and reionization (neutral -> ionized) centered z=7.7, width 1.5 in z, both standard Planck-consistent values. A floor of 0.12 is applied post-reionization (z<7.7) to represent the residual cold/molecular neutral gas retained in galaxies even after the diffuse IGM reionizes -- THIS FLOOR IS THE LEAST RIGOROUS PIECE: it is a rough order-of-magnitude estimate (literature estimates for the cold/neutral gas fraction of total baryons today range roughly 10-20%), not a precision figure. Tighten this if better fidelity is needed.

known_limitations: Or (radiation density) uses a standard literature value, not independently re-derived here.
The neutral_fraction floor (0.12) for post-reionization galactic gas is an order-of-magnitude placeholder.

Helium is not treated separately; L2/L3 treat all baryons as a single conserved species with proton-mass-equivalent number density, which is a fine approximation for number-density-level work but loses the separate H/He recombination physics (helium recombines earlier than hydrogen).

L1.name: Relic Neutrinos (Cosmic Neutrino Background)
L1.description: Decoupled from thermal plasma at t~1s (T~1 MeV). Number conserved thereafter; density is exact (1+z)^3 dilution from today's anchor value.

L2.name: Hadrons (Baryons: protons + neutrons)
L2.description: Baryon number conserved after baryogenesis (well before BBN at t~1-200s). Density is exact (1+z)^3 dilution from today's anchor value (Omega_b h^2 = 0.0224).

L3.name: Atoms (neutral hydrogen + helium)
L3.description: n2(t) * neutral_fraction(z(t)). Zero before recombination (omitted; ionized plasma has no atoms yet). Rises through recombination (z~1089), plateaus through the dark ages (~90%+ neutral), then drops sharply at reionization (z~7.7) to a residual ~12% floor representing cold galactic gas.
